dct_title_s: [Series of maps from Murphy's Biblical atlas, containing twenty-four maps, illustrative of scripture geography]
dct_description_sm: Relief shown by hachures and pictorially.
Coordinates approximate and based on Greenwich meridian.
7 bar scales on maps given in "British miles", "Roman miles", "Jewish miles", "Yards", "Cubits", "Romish miles", and "English miles".
Includes 2 indexes: References to Asia Minor -- References.
Bible citations next to sites on maps throughout.
Shows topography, drainage, boundaries, travel routes, places of historical interest.

[1] The world on Mercator's projection, showing the proportion of the other maps and the Holy Land -- [2] Settlement of Noah's descendants throughout the world -- [3] The forty years journeyings of the Israelites from Egypt to the land of Canaan -- [4] Asia Minor, Syria, Palestine & Greece, this distinguisheth the seven apocalyptic churches of Asia -- [5] Places mentioned in the Old and New Testaments with those remote from the Holy Land -- [6] Palestine or the Holy Land -- [7] Tribe of Asher -- [8] Tribe of Naphtali -- [9] Tribe of Manasseh beyond Jordan -- [10] Tribe of Zebulun -- [11] Tribe of Issachar -- [12] Tribe of Gad -- [13] Tribe of Manasseh this side Jordan -- [14] Tribe of Ephraim -- [15] Tribe of Reuben -- [16] Tribe of Benjamin -- [17] Tribe of Dan -- [18] Tribe of Simeon -- [19] Tribe of Judah part first -- [20] Tribe of Judah part second -- [21] Plan of the city of Jerusalem -- [22] Plan of the Temple of Jerusalem in the time of Christ built by Herod -- [23] The ministerial journies of our Lord & savior Jesus Christ -- [24] The countries travelled by the Apostles, showing also St. Paul's routes through Asia & Europe.
